delivered: human-proposed and human-approved broker-owned quality command sets
argv-only command execution without model-generated shell strings
targeted and full deterministic quality runs with per-repository required gates
single-repository, monorepo affected-component, and combined polyrepo integration snapshots
disposable execution copies protected by Landlock and user/PID/IPC/UTS/network namespaces
clean environments, bounded time/output/process/file/memory resources, and hashed sanitized evidence
secret, PHI, PII, privilege, test-integrity, contract-compatibility, and destructive-migration checks

quality run dossier checkpoints and append-only audit events
unresolved_risks: Same-user agents can still read broker SQLite files unless an OS-separated broker service owns runtime storage
CLI approver names are attributable audit labels, not cryptographically authenticated human identities
Scanners cannot guarantee detection of every secret, PHI, PII, privileged item, or semantic test weakening
Large and non-Python projects require explicit approved command sets and may require tuned broker-owned resource limits
Windows executables are denied because WSL namespaces and Landlock cannot confine a Windows child process
Implementation application, repair orchestration, research, DLP, cloud review, and deployment remain unavailable
next_allowed_phase: phase-5-code-and-offline-security-tests; network service installation or live fetch requires separate authorization
